final_prompt.prompt:
Create a museum-grade Chinese ethnic costume infograph in the style of a National Museum exhibition panel.

Style/medium: needs a "博物馆图鉴式" aesthetic — references in the spirit of 故宫文物图录 editorial design, 国家博物馆展陈 panel layouts, 1950s-1970s Chinese ethnographic illustration tradition; exact stylistic execution is yours
Composition: must be fixed five-zone layout — top title block, left structural breakdown zone with callout lines, upper-right material/texture zone, middle-right pattern/color symbolism zone, bottom wearing-order flowchart + core characteristics summary
Subject: must include a full-body figure in authentic ethnic costume as central focal point (or central object breakdown if costume is object-based), with detailed structural callouts and close-up insets
Scene/backdrop: must be paper-textured background in rice-white, silk-paper white, or light tea-tones with museum-grade archival quality
Lighting/mood: needs a scholarly, restrained, professional atmosphere — exact lighting treatment is yours
Color palette: must be archival and muted — rice-white, silk-paper white, light tea tones dominant with authentic costume colors as accent
Materials/textures: must include realistic fabric swatches showing weave, embroidery, metalwork, leather, or other authentic materials with texture close-ups
Text (verbatim): must include 简体中文 throughout — 主标题 + 副标题 + 导语 at top; 引线标注 for all structural components; 材质说明 / 工艺说明; 纹样寓意 / 色彩含义; 穿着顺序 / 核心特征总结. All text must be clear, regular, readable 简体中文 — no garbled characters, errors, English, or pinyin
Constraints: must preserve authentic ethnic costume structure and cultural accuracy; must be documentary/educational in tone not promotional; all annotations must correspond to real structural elements; must maintain collectible museum-panel quality
Avoid: poster aesthetics, studio photography vibes, e-commerce styling, anime/illustration styling, cosplay aesthetics, incorrect labeling, false structures, blurry text, fake materials, excessive decoration
